[[Doctor/Legends|Doctor]] '''A. Rahring''' was a [[Kel Dor/Legends|Kel Dor]] [[Xenobiologist/Legends|xenobiologist]] living in the last [[Standard decade/Legends|decades]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]].

==Biography==
Rahring led a research team to [[Dorumaa]], the only [[Moon/Legends|moon]] of [[Almas]] and, a long time previously, a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] itself. The team was trying to study the [[Dorumaan leviathan]], a strange, almost extinct, oceanic mammal. In previous years, two young leviathans had been born, but their mother had disappeared strangely. Some believed that the youngsters were the last leviathans, but others thought the mother was frozen or hibernating somewhere under the surface of Dorumaa.<ref name="r" />

He discovered that the leviathan was similar to the [[Mon Calamari Star Cruiser/Legends|Mon Calamari Star Cruiser]], which made some believe that the leviathans had been imported to [[Dac/Legends|Dac]] long ago, and then became extinct there. Later, Rahring hosted the documentary ''[[A Galaxy Befuddled]]'', showing the strange life of the leviathan.<ref name="r" />

Soon after that, one of the young leviathans disappeared. It was suspected that it had been kidnapped by [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] [[Rufus Trammel]], who likely tried to sell it to one of Rahring's fans.<ref name="r" />

==Behind the scenes==
Rahring's portrait, as shown in Wizards' webpage, had been previously used in [[Star Wars Roleplaying Game|''Star Wars Roleplaying Game: Revised Core Rulebook'']] to depict a generic [[Jedi investigator/Legends|Jedi investigator]], in the prestige class description.
